# omada_ssid (Resource)

Manages an Omada WiFi SSID. An SSID always belongs to a WLAN group; create a WLAN group that is not bound to any AP (omada_wlan_group) to stage SSIDs safely without broadcasting. Targets the Open API v1 wireless-network surface implemented by controller firmware such as 5.15.x. Requires one of: `Site Settings Manager Modify` or `Network Config Page Modify`.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Site ID and WLAN group that own the SSID. Provide these via variables or data
# sources so the values are not hard-coded in checked-in configurations.
variable "site_id" {
  type = string
}

variable "wlan_group_id" {
  type = string
}

variable "ssid_psk" {
  type      = string
  sensitive = true
}

resource "omada_ssid" "iot" {
  site_id       = var.site_id
  wlan_group_id = var.wlan_group_id
  name          = "IoT"
  security      = 3 # WPA-Personal
  band          = 3 # 2.4G + 5G
  broadcast     = true

  # Tag IoT client traffic onto the IoT VLAN (managed by omada_lan_network).
  vlan_enable = true
  vlan_id     = 30

  psk_setting = {
    psk = var.ssid_psk
  }
}

output "iot_ssid_id" {
  value = omada_ssid.iot.ssid_id
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `band` (Number) Radio band bitfield. Bit 0 = 2.4G, bit 1 = 5G, bit 2 = 6G. e.g. `7` enables 2.4G/5G/6G, `3` enables 2.4G/5G.
- `name` (String) SSID name broadcast to clients. Must contain 1 to 32 UTF-8 characters.
- `security` (Number) Security mode. `0` = None (open), `2` = WPA-Enterprise, `3` = WPA-Personal (PSK), `4` = PPSK without RADIUS, `5` = PPSK with RADIUS. Mode `3` requires a `psk_setting` block.
- `site_id` (String) Site ID that owns the SSID. Changing this forces replacement.
- `wlan_group_id` (String) WLAN group the SSID belongs to (an omada_wlan_group or an existing group ID). SSIDs cannot be moved between groups; changing this forces replacement.

### Optional

- `broadcast` (Boolean) Enable SSID broadcast. Defaults to `true`.
- `device_type` (Number) Target device bitfield. Bit 0 = EAP, bit 1 = Gateway. e.g. `3` targets both (the controller default).
- `enable_11r` (Boolean) Enable 802.11r fast roaming. Defaults to `false`.
- `guest_net_enable` (Boolean) Treat this as a guest network (isolates clients). Defaults to `false`.
- `hide_pwd` (Boolean) Hide the PSK in the controller UI/API where supported. Defaults to `false`. The provider still preserves the PSK in Terraform state regardless of this setting.
- `mlo_enable` (Boolean) Enable Wi-Fi 7 multi-link operation. Defaults to `false`.
- `pmf_mode` (Number) Protected Management Frames mode. `1` = Mandatory, `2` = Capable (default), `3` = Disable.
- `psk_setting` (Attributes) WPA-Personal key material. Required for security mode `3`; omit for open/enterprise. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--psk_setting))
- `vlan_enable` (Boolean) Tag client traffic to a VLAN. When `true`, `vlan_id` must be set. Defaults to `false`.
- `vlan_id` (Number) 802.1Q VLAN tag for this SSID. Required and only sent when `vlan_enable` is `true`. Must be in the range 1-4094.

### Read-Only

- `ssid_id` (String) SSID ID assigned by the controller. Use it (with site_id and wlan_group_id) as the import target.

<a id="nestedatt--psk_setting"></a>
### Nested Schema for `psk_setting`

Optional:

- `gik_rekey_psk_enable` (Boolean) Enable group key rekey. Defaults to `false`.
- `psk` (String, Sensitive) WPA-Personal passphrase (8-63 chars). Sensitive: it round-trips through remote state (expected) but is never printed in plan/log output. When the controller masks the key on read, the provider preserves the prior-state value.
- `psk_encryption` (Number) PSK cipher. Defaults to `3` (the controller default on 5.15.x).
- `psk_version` (Number) PSK version. Defaults to `4` (WPA2/WPA3 Personal compatibility).
